# WorkStation Quick Handoff

- Project: WorkStation front display board bring-up (ESP32-C6, PlatformIO, Arduino_GFX)
- Active env: `display_board`
- Port: COM9
- Display module under test: Hosyond MSP4022 4" SPI TFT family
- Current TFT pin map in use on C6 path: CS=10, DC=18, RST=19, SCLK=12, MISO=11, MOSI=13, BL/PWM=20
- Shared SPI devices: Touch CS=22, SD CS=15, both forced HIGH during TFT-only tests
- Current firmware state:
  - `src/display_main.cpp` includes legacy raw-SPI wake/fill helpers
  - minimal TFT-only mode is enabled in `platformio.ini`
  - current minimal loop alternates pixel format assumptions:
    - MODE A: RGB565 / `0x3A=0x55`
    - MODE B: 18-bit / `0x3A=0x66`
- Most important result:
  - panel is not fully dead
  - minimal legacy write-only path produced visible static/pixelation
  - panel wake is real, but intended framebuffer updates are not clearly accepted
- Working hypothesis at stop point:
  - module is bad, marginal, or otherwise not worth more time right now
  - continue on the alternative display board path instead of further firmware tuning on this module
- Operational notes:
  - COM9 often needs all monitors closed before upload
  - user often must unplug/replug USB, then reset after flashing to observe panel behavior

## Paste Prompt
Continue from the WorkStation display-board breakpoint. The ESP32-C6 `display_board` env on COM9 now has a known baseline in `src/display_main.cpp`: legacy raw-SPI wake, touch/SD isolation, and `MINIMAL_TFT_ONLY` A/B pixel-format testing. The Hosyond module showed visible static/pixelation on the minimal legacy path, proving partial life, but did not clearly accept intended image data. Current decision is to pause this module as likely bad/marginal and continue on the alternative display board path. Preserve the current baseline and do not reopen broad TFT experimentation unless explicitly requested.
